notes: switch(subject, pattern1, branch1[, pattern2, branch2, ...][, default]) -- patterns are wildcard-matched (*/?) against subject, case-insensitively; only the matching branch (or default) is evaluated, never the losing branches (verified via setq() side effects below). Fewer than 2 arguments returns "", not an error. EV_FMAND does NOT propagate into switch()'s own arguments even directly inside [...] forced-eval -- oracle-verified [switch(1,1,badname(1),2)] literal-falls-back to "badname(1)", it never hard-errors, unlike an ordinary eager function's arguments (see forced-eval.json); same for the dynamic-name path once the resolved name turns out to be switch().
